enrichment.summary: Kyle does a catch-up daily vlog from his Boulder home, recapping the previous day: his hamster escaped and is likely dead, he biked to work, spent the day on his porch organizing playlists and doing website work, and reflects on his casual bike commute and continuous marijuana use since Colorado legalization.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: resigned acceptance — it's probably dead, 'it's fine'
refuses to race his commute; wants to enjoy the ride and smell the flowers
casual and positive — has been continuously high since legalization, considers functionality essentially normal

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He forgot to do his daily vlog for yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His hamster escaped and he put food and water down for it.

claims.subject: hamster
claims.claim: It has to climb up a sheer wall to get out, which hamsters can't do.

claims.subject: hamster
claims.claim: If it jumps down, it could die or get lost.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thinks the hamster is probably dead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He came back from work and did research on motorcycles.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He spent the day organizing playlists for living in Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hadn't organized playlists for months.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He did a little bit of work on his website but not much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He worked on his porch all day and enjoyed it because it was warm.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He rediscovered a new part of his house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a setup with his shoes strapped to a rack, power bars, wallet, bike log, and extra parts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He forgot to plug in his device and didn't record his bike ride on Strava.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He doesn't race on Strava when biking to work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He prefers to enjoy the ride, smell the flowers, and enjoy the sunshine rather than race.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He bikes on bike paths to work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He admits he's still a little high from smoking weed the night before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hasn't been sober since weed was legalized.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He claims that even when high, he functions at 99% capacity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He says his memory and structure are not significantly affected by being high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He feels like the colors are still vibrant when he's high.
